title: We need a client portal | Bc. Ondřej Halata (halatao.cz)

seo.title: We need a client portal | Bc. Ondřej Halata (halatao.cz)
seo.description: I help companies design client portals when repeated customer communication, document sharing, and status handling should move into one clearer interface.

page.title: We need a client portal | Bc. Ondřej Halata (halatao.cz)
page.h1: We need a client portal for visibility, documents, and self-service
page.description: I help companies design client portals when repeated customer communication, document sharing, and status handling should move into one clearer interface.

hero.title: Clients need one reliable place instead of fragmented email handling
hero.subtitle: A typical situation when status questions repeat, documents are shared manually, and the company wants a better self-service layer around the service.

intro: A client portal makes sense when the company keeps repeating the same communication around service state, documents, requests, or self-service actions.
A strong first step is to choose the client interactions with the biggest operational impact and build the first version around those.

sections.title: Typical symptoms
body: Without a portal layer, support stays expensive, communication remains fragmented, and the customer experience depends too much on who has time to answer manually.
bullets: repeated customer questions about service or project status

sections.title: How I approach it
body: I help define the most important customer scenarios, the right access model, and the connection to internal workflows so the portal becomes an operational improvement, not just a visual layer.

sections.title: What a good outcome looks like
body: The goal is not a quick patch. The goal is to restore control, confidence, and a practical next step.
bullets: better visibility for customers or partners
stronger base for future client-facing features

faq.question: Is a client portal useful for a smaller company too?
faq.answer: Yes, if the same customer questions, documents, or actions keep repeating and the portal can reduce support load.

faq.question: Does the portal have to be large from day one?
faq.answer: No. A focused first version around the most common interactions is often the strongest start.

faq.question: Do we also need internal admin capability?
faq.answer: Usually yes. A valuable client portal normally depends on a sensible backoffice layer as well.

for: companies with repeated client communication around status or documents
services where self-service would reduce support load
teams that want the client layer connected to internal workflow
notFor: a portal with no connection to internal systems or process
projects expecting a visual effect with no operational change

cta.label: Explore the project fit
cta.note: A short summary of the current state and the main risk is enough to start.
